editableNewDialogue.text: Can you continue step 6 in English and keep it short?

Sure—continuing from step 6:

6. Break down goals: Split each goal into weekly/monthly tasks with clear deliverables.
7. Track progress: Review metrics regularly and note what’s working or not.
8. Use feedback: Ask for input from a manager/teammates and adjust.
9. Reflect and improve: Identify skill gaps and plan learning actions.
10. Take consistent action: Schedule time, remove blockers, and follow through.
What’s one example of a measurable job goal?
Example: “Reduce average customer ticket response time from 24 hours to 12 hours within the next 60 days.”
Also, random question: what does SMART stand for again?
SMART = Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound.
I saw a headline about a lighthouse being rebuilt somewhere—do lighthouses still matter?

They can. Many are historic landmarks and still serve as backups or navigation aids in certain coastal areas, especially where conditions are hazardous. Even when modern GPS is primary, physical beacons and charts remain part of redundancy planning in maritime safety.
I’m writing my OKRs for this quarter, and my manager keeps asking me to show a simple dashboard—like % complete each week. Any easy way to track it?

Yes—keep it lightweight. A simple weekly tracker can be a Google Sheet/Excel with columns like: Objective, Key Result, baseline, target, owner, weekly check-in date, current value, % to target, blockers, next actions. If you want it visual, add a progress bar (conditional formatting) and a small chart per KR. If you tell me your role/team and 2–3 KRs, I can draft a template.
Earlier I kept asking 「譯中文 / 譯繁體中文」 just because I was sharing it with my coworker. For myself, please answer in English only—no Chinese (neither Simplified nor Traditional).
Got it—I'll respond in English only from now on, and I won’t include Chinese translations unless you explicitly ask for them.
